_comment: NL Observability Program Wave O, task O.1 (specs-o §O.0.3 contract C3 / §O.1 deliverable 4). Seeded with ALL signal-ledger event types observed in this repo as of Wave O batch 1: the 8 pre-existing classes (ADR 058 D6 + Wave E/F callers) plus the 10 new Wave-O lifecycle/spawn/task/turn-trace classes (contract C2). Every entry names >=1 REAL consumer that exists on disk today (grep-verified against this repo, not aspirational) EXCEPT where a consumer is O.3/O.6-owned and does not exist yet in this batch — those name the future consumer explicitly per the O.0.3 instruction ('the C4/C5 consumers count once O.3 lands -- name them now, that is the point of the map'), tagged not-yet-landed so O.6's check_obs_consumer_map doctor predicate can distinguish 'named, pending a later Wave-O task' from 'genuinely unmapped'. Law 2 (every event type has >=1 named consumer) is the invariant this file exists to satisfy; O.6 owns the enforcing doctor check (check_obs_consumer_map).

consumers: writer:signal-ledger-flush.sh (self-emits its own segment terminator; read back by any tailing consumer as an ordinary ledger line)
gate-demotion:gate-demotion.sh (self-consumes for idempotency — a gate already demoted is not re-demoted)
tool-call-counter:tool-call-counter.sh (self-emits threshold crossings; read back by any tailing consumer)

cli:nl-why (O.3, not-yet-landed this batch — od_why per contract C4)

digest:feed_ledger_summary (NEVER EMITTED as of O.1 — no mechanical tap exists for background-dispatch completion; see workstreams-emit.sh's own documented COMPLETION-SIGNAL CEILING and this task's report-back. Entry present to satisfy law 2's 'every event type in C2 has a named consumer' even though the writer side is an honest, documented gap, not yet a real emission.)
cli:nl-why (O.3, not-yet-landed this batch — od_why per contract C4, per-Stop hook-timing breakdown)
